title: How to Build a Master Board from Multiple Boards (and Automatically Label Items by Board)

posts.author: Fortimus_Team (Fortimus)
posts.cookedHtml:
A setup I see quite often in teams is this:

Each team manages their work in their own board, but leadership still needs a single place to get a high-level view of everything happening across projects.

Sales has their board.
Operations has theirs.
Product and engineering have theirs as well.

The challenge is creating a master board that shows important items from these team boards without forcing everyone to work in one large board.

One way teams approach this is by duplicating selected items into a central board that acts as a high-level overview. This allows teams to continue working in their own boards while leadership can track key work in one place, although you do have to make the changes on both sides.

A few things teams typically include in a master board:

  • Key milestones

  • High priority tasks

  • Client deliverables

  • Cross-team work

We ran into this problem frequently, which is why we built Mirror Item Multiple Boards. It allows the same item to appear in multiple boards while keeping column values, updates, and subitems in sync.

151920×960 254 KB

181920×960 168 KB

We recently added a feature for this workflow:
when an item is mirrored into the Master Board, the name of the original board can automatically be added as a dropdown label. This makes it easier to filter items and quickly see where each item came from.

image1380×354 25.6 KB

Curious how others here structure their master boards across teams. Would love to hear how you approach it.

posts.author: Kai_Pioneera (Kai from Pioneera)
posts.cookedHtml:
Great overview of the master board setup! This is one of the most common challenges teams face as they scale in monday.com.

For anyone exploring options in this space, another app worth checking out is Same Item Multiple Boards (SIMB) by Pioneera. It takes a similar approach - letting the same item live on multiple boards with full two-way syncing of columns, updates, and subitems.

One difference worth noting is selective field syncing - you can choose exactly which columns sync between boards instead of syncing everything. This is helpful when different teams need to see the same item but track different information on their respective boards.

SIMB is the #1 rated app in this category with 8,600+ installs. Worth comparing both options to see which workflow fits your team best!

posts.author: Fortimus_Team (Fortimus)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for sharing, Kai!

Just to clarify for anyone comparing options — Mirror Item Multiple Boards also supports selective column syncing, so you can choose which fields stay synced between boards. We also support a few additional workflows like one-way syncing, excluding specific columns, and auto-filling connect board columns when items are mirrored.

image1372×1278 91.3 KB

Mirror Item Multiple Boards is currently listed as Editor’s Choice in the monday marketplace.

As mentioned, it’s always good to compare a couple of options and see which setup works best for your workflow!
